q:
What does E5 refer to?

A. The octane content of the petrol.
B. The added bio-ethanol.
e: Since 2019, fuel is primarily designated by a name that indicates the maximum amount of bioethanol mixed in. Bioethanol is an alcohol made by fermenting sugar, corn or wheat. This is therefore a form of renewable energy.
clean_q:
What does E5 refer to?
A. The octane content of the petrol.
B. The added bio-ethanol.
clean_e: Since 2019, fuel is primarily designated by a name that indicates the maximum amount of bioethanol mixed in. Bioethanol is an alcohol made by fermenting sugar, corn or wheat. This is therefore a form of renewable energy.

q:
Which fuel is the MOST polluting or least environmentally friendly?

A. White diesel.
B. Red diesel.
C. Low sulfur diesel.
e:
LESSON 30 – ‘Environment’.
Red diesel contains 200 times more particles sulfur than white diesel.
Red diesel you may not use for a car.
Low sulfur diesel is environmentally friendly.
clean_q:
Which fuel is the MOST polluting or least environmentally friendly?
A. White diesel.
B. Red diesel.
C. Low sulfur diesel.
clean_e:
LESSON 30 – ‘Environment’.  
Red diesel contains 200 times more particles sulfur than white diesel.
Red diesel you may not use for a car.
Low sulfur diesel is environmentally friendly.

q:
When is a car the most environmentally friendly?

A. With a high eco-score.
B. With a low eco-score.
e:
LESSON 30 – “Environment”.
Eco-score is expressed with a number between 1 and 100.
The higher, the more environmentally friendly.

A. With a high eco-score.

q:
You wait for a level crossing with your car motor in freewheel mode.
Can you repeatedly give some gas?

A. No.
B. Yes.
C. Yes, only in the winter to keep warm the engine.
e:
LESSON 30 – ‘Environment’.
For example, if you wait for a red light with a motor in freewheel mode, repetitive gas (brum brum ... ... broeoemm ...) is not allowed. Not even in the winter to keep warm the engine.

q:
What is ‘Autodelen’ (Dutch) or ‘Voitures partagées’ (French)?

A. With multiple people use the car at the same time to reach a destination.
B. Occasional use of a car in advance captured moments via reservation.
e:
LESSON 30 – ‘Environment’.
Who shares a car with several people, make use of a car at a different time. You use the car when you need the car. If you don’t need the car, someone else can drive.

What is ‘Autodelen’ (Dutch) or ‘Voitures partagées’ (French)?

B. Occasional use of a car in advance captured moments via reservation.
clean_e:
LESSON 30 – ‘Environment’.  
Who shares a car with several people, make use of a car at a different time. You use the car when you need the car. If you don’t need the car, someone else can drive.

q:
Is driving at too high an engine speed also harmful to the environment?

A. No.
B. Yes.
e:
LESSON 30
When you let your engine run unnecessarily at high revs, it consumes more fuel and emits more CO₂ and nitrogen oxides. This is harmful to the environment and increases engine wear, leading to higher maintenance costs.
clean_q:
Is driving at too high an engine speed also harmful to the environment?
A. No.
B. Yes.
clean_e:
LESSON 30

q:
When must you take this sign into account?

A. Only on weekdays, 24 hours a day.
B. Only on weekdays, including Saturday, from 06:00 to 20:00.
C. Every day of the week, 24 hours a day.
e:
LESSON 30
These signs indicate the start and end of a low-emission zone (LEZ).
A low-emission zone is a defined area where certain vehicles are not allowed because they emit too many harmful substances.
A LEZ applies every day of the week, 24 hours a day, unless stated otherwise.
Each city or municipality can decide where a LEZ is introduced.
clean_q:
When must you take this sign into account?
A. Only on weekdays, 24 hours a day.
B. Only on weekdays, including Saturday, from 06:00 to 20:00.
C. Every day of the week, 24 hours a day.
clean_e:
LESSON 30

A LEZ applies every day of the week, 24 hours a day, unless stated otherwise.
Each city or municipality can decide where a LEZ is introduced.

q:
This sign means ...

A. Start of a low emission zone.
B. Zone where diesels are banned.
C. Zone where a catalyst is mandatory in the car.
e:
LES 30 – ‘Environment’.
Road authorities have the ability to ban polluting vehicles from certain areas, with a view to improving air quality.
